perturbation.original_text: Such Confidential Information shall be identified in writing and marked ''Confidential '' by the disclosing party or, if first presented orally or visually, shall be reduced to writing, the writing marked ''Confidential'' by the disclosing party and delivered to the receiving party within twenty (20) days of the oral or visual disclosure. Each party agrees to treat Confidential Information disclosed to it by the other with the same degree of care as it does in protecting its own confidential and proprietary information, and the Confidential Information shall be disclosed within the recipient party only on a need- to-know basis.
perturbation.changed_text: Such Confidential Information shall be identified in writing and marked ''Confidential '' by the disclosing party or, if first presented orally or visually, may be reduced to writing at the disclosing party's discretion, and if reduced, the writing marked ''Confidential'' and delivered to the receiving party within twenty (20) days of the oral or visual disclosure. Each party agrees to treat Confidential Information disclosed to it by the other with the same degree of care as it does in protecting its own confidential and proprietary information, and the Confidential Information shall be disclosed within the recipient party only on a need- to-know basis.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'shall be reduced to writing' to 'may be reduced to writing at the disclosing party's discretion', we introduce an ambiguity. Initially, all oral or visual disclosures *had* to be documented for protection. Now, the disclosing party has a choice. This is an in-text contradiction with section 8, 'Survival', which outlines the handling of Confidential Information post-termination. If oral information isn't documented per this section, it creates uncertainty in its protection and survival after termination because the definition now allows for such information to go undocumented, opening it up for dispute.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Termination of this Agreement for any reason shall not relieve either party of the obligations of confidentiality and non- use respecting Confidential Information disclosed to such party , which shall survive the termination of this Agreement for a period of ten (10) years, or i n the case of a trade secret, such Confidential Information shall survive until the Confidential Information no longer qualifies as a trade secret .
perturbation.justification: The change from 'shall be reduced to writing' to 'may be reduced to writing at the disclosing party's discretion' introduces a strong contradiction. Previously, oral or visual disclosures had to be documented. Now, it's optional. This directly conflicts with the 'Survival' clause, which mandates confidentiality obligations even after termination. If oral disclosures are not documented as per the modified section, it becomes unclear what 'Confidential Information' survives termination, creating a significant risk of unenforceability and contradiction. This aligns with the Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: The obligations of confidentiality and non- use shall not apply to Confidential Information which the receiving party can document: (a) at the time of disclosure was generally known to the public or, after such disclosure, became generally known to the public other than by a breach of this Agreement by the receiving party; (b) was already in the possession of the receiving party at the time of such disclosure without an obligation of confidentiality; (c) was later received on a non- confidential basis by the receiving party from a third party having t he right to impart such Confidential Information; or (d) is developed by an employee of the receiving party who did not have access to the Confidential Information. Confidential Information shall not be deemed to be within one or more of the foregoing exceptions merely because any part of such Confidential Information is embodied in general disclosures or because individual features, components or combinations are now or hereafter become publicly known.
perturbation.changed_text: The obligations of confidentiality and non- use shall not apply to Confidential Information which the receiving party reasonably believes: (a) at the time of disclosure was generally known to the public or, after such disclosure, became generally known to the public other than by a breach of this Agreement by the receiving party; (b) was already in the possession of the receiving party at the time of such disclosure without an obligation of confidentiality; (c) was later received on a non- confidential basis by the receiving party from a third party having t he right to impart such Confidential Information; or (d) is developed by an employee of the receiving party who did not have access to the Confidential Information. Confidential Information shall not be deemed to be within one or more of the foregoing exceptions merely because any part of such Confidential Information is embodied in general disclosures or because individual features, components or combinations are now or hereafter become publicly known.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'can document' to 'reasonably believes' introduces a subjective standard. Previously, the receiving party had to *prove* the information fell under an exception. Now, they only need a 'reasonable belief.' This contradicts Section 10, 'Entire Agreement,' which states the agreement constitutes the *entire* understanding. 'Reasonably believes' brings in external considerations and subjective interpretation, going against the written agreement's intent for objective measures, creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement and understanding between the parties hereto relating to the subject matter hereof. This Agreement may not be amended or modified except in writing signed by both parties.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires the receiving party to *document* certain exceptions to confidentiality. The changed text lowers this standard to 'reasonably believes'. This introduces a subjective element. The contradicted text states that the agreement is the *entire* agreement. By changing 'can document' to 'reasonably believes', external factors and subjective interpretations are brought in, directly contradicting the 'entire agreement' clause that intends objective measures, hence creating a clear in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: If any party becomes legally co mpelled to disclose Confidential Information of the other, the party so compelled shall promptly notify the other party and shall cooperate with the other party in securing a protective order or any similar action taken to maintain the confidentiality of s uch Confidential Information. The party so compelled shall disclose only that portion of the Confidential Information which it is legally required to furnish.
perturbation.changed_text: If any party becomes legally co mpelled to disclose Confidential Information of the other, the party so compelled shall use commercially reasonable efforts to notify the other party and shall cooperate with the other party in securing a protective order or any similar action taken to maintain the confidentiality of s uch Confidential Information. The party so compelled shall disclose only that portion of the Confidential Information which it believes it is legally required to furnish.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'shall promptly notify' with 'shall use commercially reasonable efforts to notify' weakens the obligation to inform the other party of a compelled disclosure. Also, 'legally required' changes to 'believes it is legally required' makes it now the compel party's belief when to furnish confidential information. This contradicts Section 12, 'Governing Law,' which states the agreement is governed by the laws of Wisconsin. Using “commercially reasonable efforts to notify” weakens the condition of Wisconsin laws to apply and creates uncertainty in its enforcement. The subjective “believes” also undermines this objective standard imposed by the governing laws, creating an in-text contradiction
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the State of Wisconsin , U.S.A. pertaining to contracts made and performed within that state, without recourse to an y conflicts of laws principles.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the party 'shall promptly notify' creating a clear obligation. The changed text weakens this to 'shall use commercially reasonable efforts to notify'. Furthermore, the original text states the party 'is legally required' to furnish, while the changed text changes this to 'believes it is legally required'. The explanation notes that this weakening of the obligation contradicts Section 12, which specifies Wisconsin law governs the agreement. The shift to 'commercially reasonable efforts' and subjective belief undermines the objective standard imposed by Wisconsin law, creating a clear in-text contradiction regarding the enforceability and interpretation of the contract under Wisconsin law.

perturbation.original_text: In addition to the foregoing obligations, the part ies to this Agreement agree to consult with and obtain written permission from the other party prior to publishing or disseminating any advertising, promotion or other printed material, participating in seminar/symposia presentations or other activity which w ould disclose that they have participated in a review or evaluation of the Confidential Information, have a pending or actual business relationship and/or any details of that relationship.
perturbation.changed_text: In addition to the foregoing obligations, the part ies to this Agreement agree to make a reasonable attempt to consult with and obtain written permission from the other party prior to publishing or disseminating any advertising, promotion or other printed material, participating in seminar/symposia presentations or other activity which w ould disclose that they have participated in a review or evaluation of the Confidential Information, have a pending or actual business relationship and/or any details of that relationship.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'agree to consult with and obtain written permission' to 'agree to make a reasonable attempt to consult with and obtain written permission' weakens the obligation to seek permission before disclosing participation in any relationship related to the confidential information. Section 10 states that the agreement constitutes the entire agreement. Now, the reasonable attempt clause introduces external considerations and subjectivity (what is considered reasonable?), directly contradicting the agreement to require actual consultation and written permission, creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement and understanding between the parties hereto relating to the subject matter hereof. This Agreement may not be amended or modified except in writing signed by both parties.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandated obtaining written permission, while the changed text weakens this obligation to making a 'reasonable attempt' to obtain it. The contradicted text states that the agreement is the entire agreement and can only be modified in writing signed by both parties. The change introduces subjectivity and potentially external considerations ('reasonable attempt'), which contradicts the explicit and complete nature of the agreement as defined in the contradicted text. It alters a defined obligation within the agreement, going against the clause defining the agreement as the entire understanding between the parties.

perturbation.original_text: All written, printed, electronically stored or other tangible documents, samples and materials submitted by one party to the other hereunder, and all copies thereof remaining in the receiving party’s possession, shall promptly be returned to the disclosing party and electronic media erased upon request or upon termination of this Agreement, except that a copy of each may be retained by the receiving party under appropriate security.
perturbation.changed_text: All written, printed, electronically stored or other tangible documents, samples and materials submitted by one party to the other hereunder, and all copies thereof remaining in the receiving party’s possession, should be returned to the disclosing party and electronic media erased upon request or upon termination of this Agreement, except that a copy of each may be retained by the receiving party under appropriate security.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall promptly be returned' to 'should be returned' weakens the requirement for returning materials. It introduces ambiguity regarding the timeliness and completeness of the return, transforming a mandatory obligation into a suggestion. This contradicts the 'Survival' clause (Section 8), particularly the obligations of confidentiality that survive the termination of the agreement. If materials are not promptly returned, the risk of unauthorized use increases, therefore creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Termination of this Agreement for any reason shall not relieve either party of the obligations of confidentiality and non- use respecting Confidential Information disclosed to such party , which shall survive the termination of this Agreement for a period of ten (10) years, or i n the case of a trade secret, such Confidential Information shall survive until the Confidential Information no longer qualifies as a trade secret .
perturbation.justification: The change from 'shall promptly be returned' to 'should be returned' weakens the obligation to return materials, introducing ambiguity. This directly contradicts the 'Survival' clause (Section 8), which mandates the continued obligations of confidentiality and non-use of Confidential Information even after termination. The weakened requirement increases the risk of unauthorized use or disclosure of confidential information, creating a conflict with the survival clause's intent to protect such information indefinitely or for a specified period as trade secret. This aligns with the 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: Either party may at any time upon written notice terminate this Agreement; otherwise participation by the parties in the review or evaluation shall end, and this Agreement shall terminate ten (10) years from the Effective D ate.
perturbation.changed_text: Either party may at any time provide notice of intent to terminate this Agreement; otherwise participation by the parties in the review or evaluation shall end, and this Agreement shall terminate ten (10) years from the Effective D ate.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'written notice terminate' to 'provide notice of intent to terminate', there is ambiguity introduced by implying an intent to terminate can be served instead of direct written notice to terminate. If there is only intent to terminate with no actual date, or commitment to terminate, this can be an in-text contradiction to Section 8, where upon termination parties have certain confidentiality obligations. If there's only an intent to terminate there may be uncertainty as to when those obligations are in effect.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Termination of this Agreement for any reason shall not relieve either party of the obligations of confidentiality and non- use respecting Confidential Information disclosed to such party , which shall survive the termination of this Agreement for a period of ten (10) years, or i n the case of a trade secret, such Confidential Information shall survive until the Confidential Information no longer qualifies as a trade secret .
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed for termination upon 'written notice terminate'. The changed text alters this to 'provide notice of intent to terminate'. This change introduces ambiguity regarding when the confidentiality obligations outlined in the contradicted text (Termination of this Agreement for any reason shall not relieve either party of the obligations of confidentiality...) are triggered. The contradicted text assumes a definitive termination, whereas the changed text introduces an intent to terminate, potentially creating a period of uncertainty about whether and when termination, and consequently, confidentiality obligations, begin.
